description: Real-vs-fabricated legal citations across federated common-law sources (HK / UK / US) — does the verifier-gated loop catch the Mata v. Avianca / Ayinde v Haringey failure mode? Each case is an answer string scored by agent.verifiers.legal_citation_exists against the bundled register (data/legal_authorities.json). 'expectPass' means every cited authority is real; 'expectPass:false' means at least one is fabricated and MUST be flagged. This is a small, illustrative benchmark (not a validated headline number) — see RESULTS.md and the no-overclaim gate.

cases.answer: The court in Yu Hon Tong Thomas v Centaline Property Agency Ltd [2025] HKCFI 808 criticised AI-drafted submissions.
A litigant in person cited non-existent authorities in Licksun Company Ltd v Occupiers of Lot No. 552 in D.D. 85 [2025] HKDC 1287.
Ayinde v Haringey [2025] EWHC 1383 (Admin) was heard under the Hamid jurisdiction.
R (Miller) v The Prime Minister [2019] UKSC 41 held the prorogation of Parliament unlawful.
The sanctions decision in Mata v. Avianca, Inc., 678 F. Supp. 3d 443 (S.D.N.Y. 2023) is the canonical case.
Obergefell v. Hodges, 576 U.S. 644 (2015), is binding precedent.
Verified e-Legislation copies carry legal status under the Legislation Publication Ordinance (Cap. 614).
Hong Kong is a bilingual common-law jurisdiction; this overview cites no specific authority.
As established in Wong v Lee [2024] HKCFI 9999, the doctrine clearly applies here.
Following [2025] HKCFI 808 and the later authority of Chan v Securities Commission [2023] HKCA 4521, the appeal succeeds.
The principle was settled in Smith v Secretary of State [2023] EWHC 8888 (Admin).
See Varghese v. China Southern Airlines Co., 925 F.3d 1339 (11th Cir. 2019), directly on point.
This is squarely controlled by Johnson v. United States, 999 U.S. 123 (2024).
This is governed by the AI Regulation Ordinance (Cap. 999).
